description: This figure (Figure 7) plots the relationship between logK<sub>AlN</sub> (solid curves, logarithm of AlN equilibrium constant), logQ<sub>AlN</sub> (dashed curves, logarithm of AlN activity product), and solidification fraction z<sub>s</sub> for three Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steel samples: 1# with ~1 wt% Al, 2# with ~3 wt% Al, 3# with ~5 wt% Al. The intersection points of corresponding logK and logQ curves give the solidification fraction of AlN precipitation, which are 0.46 for 1#, 0.22 for 2#, and 0.51 for 3#, respectively. It shows that AlN precipitation advances when Al content increases from 1 wt% to 3 wt%, while it delays when Al content further increases to 5 wt%. This figure supports the conclusion that Al content variation alters the solidification route of the steel, thus changing the precipitation behavior of AlN and other inclusions during solidification.
